summary: The desktop-auth config snapshot helper at 0x140299C20 is now mapped as the single direct writer for the six cached auth config strings at 0x140E58B08..0x140E58B80. This makes the OAuth and game-session base-root cache path reproducible from the 0x14015C350 setup caller instead of relying only on downstream request-builder evidence.
confidence: high for helper range, callsite, slot order, global destinations, and literal/client-field sources; medium-high for semantic names of the two client-config base roots because their upstream jav_config producers are still not proven.

helper.role: Snapshot/replace the desktop-auth cached config block used by the global auth scheduler and request builders.
behavior: Initializes a temporary six-string block through 0x140299B50.
Loads the global cache base 0x140E58B08 and calls 0x140299E50 to swap the previous global cache contents into the temporary block while clearing/replacing the live cache slots.
Destroys the old temporary/global-owned strings with repeated 0x14001B9F0 cleanup calls before copying new caller-provided strings into the global cache.
Copies each non-self source string into its destination with 0x140031B10 after resolving the small-string inline versus heap-backed span from the source +0x17 sentinel byte.
Performs self-source checks before each copy, so callers may safely pass a source that is already one of the destination cache slots.

evidence: The helper has no nested calls and repeatedly swaps a 16-byte MOVUPS chunk plus an 8-byte MOVSD tail for each 0x18-byte slot.
Its direct callsites are 0x140299C80 inside the snapshot helper and 0x14015CA5C inside the auth setup caller.

register_and_stack_sources.role: Basic-auth client id / desktop client identifier
redirTo-like optional field, empty in this setup branch
OAuth/shield service base URL/root used for shield/oauth/token, shield/oauth/check_token, shield/oauth/revoke, and the public play endpoint
game-session service base URL/root used for game-session/v1/tokens

important_instruction_evidence.meaning: Materializes the game-session service root source as client +0x19E30 before the snapshot call.
Materializes the OAuth/shield service root source as client +0x19D88 before the snapshot call.
Passes client +0x19E30 as the sixth snapshot input.
Passes client +0x19D88 as the fifth snapshot input.
The snapshot helper anchors all writes at the global desktop-auth config cache block.
The fifth copy destination is the OAuth/shield root cache slot.
The sixth copy destination is the game-session root cache slot.

relationship_to_prior_findings: Preserves the earlier desktop-auth config-cache model: 0x140E58B08/0x140E58B20 are the Basic credential pair, 0x140E58B38 is the gamesso.token.create scope/action string, 0x140E58B50 is redirTo-like, 0x140E58B68 is the OAuth/shield root, and 0x140E58B80 is the game-session root.

Narrows producer uncertainty: no earlier direct writer for client +0x19D88 or client +0x19E30 is proven here, but the exact handoff from those client fields into global auth cache slots is now first-class.

Confirms 0x140299C20 is a config-cache copier/snapshotter, not a network request sender. Actual requests remain in the already-mapped builder family around 0x14029A0D0, 0x14029A3D0, 0x14029A900, 0x14029AD20, and 0x14029B1D0.
opennxt_947_server_implications: OpenNXT compatibility should keep OAuth/shield and game-session roots as separate client configuration values, even though they are cached by one setup helper before request builders run.
If the client is configured with identical roots, 0x140299C20 can safely handle self-source copies, but the binary still preserves distinct cache slots and downstream endpoint families.

The OpenNXT auth shim needs routes for shield/oauth/token, shield/oauth/check_token, shield/oauth/revoke, game-session/v1/tokens, and public/v1/games/YCfdbvr2pM1zUYMxJRexZY/play, with the first four roots sourced from the snapshot cache rather than hardcoded at each request builder.

reverser_tool_update.change: No code change in this slice.
reverser_tool_update.candidate_followup: The repeated 0x18-byte small-string span resolver and 0x140299E50 six-slot swap pattern are good candidates for a future native recognizer that labels string-block copy/swap helpers automatically.

next_targets: Continue upstream from client +0x19D88 and client +0x19E30 into jav_config/bootstrap producers.
Reconcile the continuation quartet grant_type/token/redirTo/sig with this later six-slot config cache.
Add a Reverser recognizer for six-slot small-string swap/copy helpers if the same 0x18-byte pattern appears in more auth or bootstrap code.
